mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate broad economic strength and rising aggregate demand, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and thus positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is near the zero lower bound, highly accommodative monetary policy boosts asset valuations by lowering the discount rate on future cash flows and encouraging risk-taking.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both retail sales and housing starts signals a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, leading to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
failure_condition: The model fails to show negative returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate over a short period signals a rapid deterioration in the labor market, which precedes corporate earnings declines and triggers negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of the months following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a healthy supply side of the economy, translating into strong corporate revenues and positive short-term equity returns as production meets demand.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 1-month INDPRO changes and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is accelerating while real GDP growth is decelerating, the economy is in a stagflationary environment. This compresses corporate profit margins and increases discount rates, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL > 4% AND quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized
failure_condition: The S&P 500 average 60-day return is positive during periods where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A technical recession, defined as two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth, confirms a severe economic downturn that leads investors to de-risk portfolios, causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q change < 0 for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following the confirmation of a technical recession is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading that significantly exceeds the trailing 12-month average signals accelerating price pressures, increasing the probability of future monetary tightening, which leads to negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL change > 1.5x the trailing 12-month average change
failure_condition: The hit rate of predicting negative 5-day returns following a trigger event is less than 55% over a 10-year period.
